comments.body: Code LANDED on main (e6f3283a0). Close BLOCKED by completion-verifier on the 'unify direction logic into ONE helper' AC — the inbound path was unified into link_direction.py but outbound_links.py + acli_graph.py kept their own copies. CLOSE 4b59 once 778 lands + verifier passes.

compiled_state.description:
## Why / Summary

The inbound link differ maps Jira `Blocks` issuelinks to the INVERTED rebar relation
(`blocks`<->`depends_on`). Latent since f6687adf4 (2026-06-15); UNMASKED when the
outbound direction fix (6b25dbead/43d154523) corrected the write path — the two
inversions had been cancelling on round-trip. Result: ~815 inbound link applies/pass
are cycle-rejected by rebar's guard, and the reconciler never converges.

## Root cause (proven, LIVE-validated)

- `_diff_links_inbound` (inbound_differ.py:519-527) AND `_diff_link_removals`
  (outbound_links.py:198-203) both invert inward/outward -> relation.
- LIVE Jira ground truth: REB-458 ("0250 blocks ba7e") carries `outwardIssue: REB-461`
  with `type.outward == "blocks"`. So **outwardIssue+Blocks => rebar `blocks`;
  inwardIssue+Blocks => rebar `depends_on`.** The code does the OPPOSITE (verified by
  running the current `_diff_links_inbound` on real REB-461 JSON — it emits the
  cycle-causing inverse).

## Chronic-pattern guardrails (this fix MUST NOT repeat prior failures)

Prior link fixes (3f04, c8ed, 3b86) each corrected ONE seam and were validated by a
ROUND-TRIP / self-consistency oracle that is invariant under double inversion — so the
mirror stayed broken and every audit read through the inversion. This fix must:

- [ ] Fix BOTH mirror sites in lockstep (inbound add AND removal path).
- [ ] UNIFY the duplicated direction logic into ONE helper (the inward/outward->relation
      mapping is copy-pasted in inbound_differ.py and outbound_links.py; `_JIRA_LINK_TO_RELATION`
      is re-declared in acli_graph.py:53, inbound_differ.py:459). One source of truth so the
      next inversion cannot reappear on an untouched copy.
- [ ] Assert ABSOLUTE direction pinned to a captured LIVE-JIRA ground-truth fixture
      (real REB-458/461 issuelinks JSON), NOT round-trip agreement. A test that asserts
      "outbound<->inbound agree" is banned here — it passes on inverted code.

## The completeness requirement (a pure branch-swap is NOT sufficient)

Measured on 17 real tickets: a pure swap eliminates the 30 cycle-rejections but then
emits ~all MIRROR-adds (the counterpart already owns the edge; ~400 spurious deps
store-wide). The fix must add INVERSE-AWARE CROSS-TICKET dedup: emit an inbound link
only if the edge X<->Y is absent in rebar in EITHER equivalent form (X has (blocks,Y)
OR Y has (depends_on,X); and the relates_to symmetric case). `compute_inbound_mutations`
already receives `local_tickets_by_id`, so the counterpart's deps are available.

- [ ] The dedup map MUST include the FULL local set (incl. archived tickets) — a residual
      mirror to archived cf10 in the live prototype was ONLY a map-exclusion artifact;
      cf10 owns the inverse edge. Verify `local_tickets_by_id` completeness or the fix
      re-emits mirrors to archived counterparts.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Local ticket A has dep `A blocks B`; both bound to Jira; outbound has (correctly)
   written REB-A outward Blocks -> REB-B.
2. Run inbound diff (or a reconcile pass).
3. Observe: inbound emits `A depends_on B` (inverted) -> rebar cycle-guard rejects it
   ("would create a cycle"). Expected: 0 inbound emits (edge already synced).

- Expected: inbound emits 0 link mutations for an already-synced link (either direction).
- Actual: inbound emits the inverted relation, cycle-rejected every pass.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A `_diff_links_inbound` unit test on the CAPTURED LIVE REB-458 issuelinks JSON
      (outwardIssue Blocks) asserts relation == `blocks`; on REB-461 JSON (inwardIssue
      Blocks) asserts relation == `depends_on`. RED on current code. Proving command:
      `pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/ -k link_direction -q`.
- [ ] A `_diff_links_inbound` test with the counterpart owning the inverse edge asserts
      0 emitted mutations (inverse-aware dedup). RED on a pure-swap fix.
- [ ] The removal-path mirror (`_diff_link_removals`) has an equivalent absolute-direction
      test. RED on current code.
- [ ] Existing tests that encode the reversed convention (test_reconcile_roundtrip.py:522,
      test_link_dedup.py:198) are corrected to the live-Jira shape and pass.
- [ ] LIVE fix-verification (pre-land): the fixed inbound differ, run against real REB
      issuelinks for the ~815 affected pairs, emits 0 cycle-rejecting mutations and 0
      mirror-adds (prototype already validated: converges to 0 with the full local map).
- [ ] `make test` passes; no new external dependency.

## Notes
P2 of epic 58b0. Blocked-independent of P1. The observability promotion (separate child)
should land AFTER this so the reconciler isn't flipped to fail-loud while this noise exists.
compiled_state.title: Inbound link differ inverts blocks<->depends_on (unmasked by outbound fix); needs inverse-aware cross-ticket dedup
